tree.title: What should I listen to right now?
tree.description: A decision tree to help you find the right music for the moment.

branch.content: What is your energy level right now?
Do you want something with vocals or mostly instrumental?
branch.fork: Vocals, give me something with a voice
Mostly instrumental, let the beats talk

branch.content: Hip hop or electronic?
Rowdy or sharp and lyrical?
branch.fork: Sharp, clever, something to actually listen to

branch.content: Beastie Boys. Licensed to Ill or Paul's Boutique depending on how chaotic you want it. Both are essential.
De La Soul. Stakes Is High or 3 Feet High and Rising. Witty, layered, aged brilliantly.
Breaks with attitude. The Freestylers or Stanton Warriors — big sound, heavy swing, UK rave energy.
Fast and percussive, or heavy and bass-driven?
What flavour of fast?
Ed Rush and Optical. Virus Vaults or Wormhole. Neurofunk at its most surgical — cold, precise, menacing.
DJ Hype. FabricLive 03. Jump-up DnB built for a room full of people. Relentless energy from start to finish.
Turntablism or broken beat?
DJ Craze. Girls On The Floor or any of his DMC sets. Scratch technique as performance — nobody sounds like him.
Deekline and Ed Solo. High energy breaks with a cheeky edge. Booty Breaks Volume 1 is the place to start.
Do you want something electronic or something with more organic texture?
Liquid and melodic, or rolling and steady?
What mood within liquid?
Calibre or Commix. Both sit in that space where DnB becomes something closer to jazz. FabricLive 68 is a masterclass.
Marcus Intalex. FabricLive 35. Sophisticated, restrained liquid DnB — the kind that sounds best at 2am.
Forward momentum or floating?
DJ Marky or Aphrodite. FabricLive 55 for Marky. Brazilian funk influences baked into liquid DnB — irresistibly groovy.
LTJ Bukem. FabricLive 46 or Logical Progression. The blueprint for atmospheric DnB. Close your eyes and let it carry you.
UK Garage and Bassline, or hip hop-influenced breaks?
Classic UKG or modern Bassline?
DJ Q on FabricLive 99 covers the whole arc. Or dig into the Nkogliaz Speed Garage mix for a pure early 2000s feel.
Dj St!cky Freeparty Filth series. Raw, functional, no-frills bassline and jackin house built for warehouses.
Tittsworth or Y4K DJ Icey. American breaks with a hip hop sensibility — choppy edits, thick bass, lots of swagger.
Abstract and textured, or minimal and repetitive?
branch.fork: Abstract, I want something to get lost in
Minimal, something that fades into the background

branch.content: Cinematic or fragmented and strange?
Amon Tobin. Out From Out Where or Supermodified. Dense layered compositions that sound like film scores from a parallel universe.
Clams Casino. His instrumentals mixtapes are beat music at its most atmospheric — woozy, hazy, hypnotic.
Electronic or more beat-driven?
Luomo. Vocalcity. Finnish minimal house with a distinctly human quality — warm, understated, endlessly listenable.
Autonomic era DnB. FabricLive 50 or Seba mixes. Half-step DnB stripped to its skeleton — meditative and forward at the same time.
